questions.q: In which Part of the Constitution are the Fundamental Duties contained?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties were added as Part IV-A of the Constitution.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties were added to the Constitution by which amendment?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties were inserted by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment in 1976.

questions.q: Which single article of the Constitution lists the Fundamental Duties?
questions.explanation: All Fundamental Duties are enumerated in Article 51A.

questions.q: How many Fundamental Duties does the list currently contain?
questions.explanation: The list of Fundamental Duties now contains eleven duties.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties are obligations addressed primarily to whom?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties are obligations laid down for the citizens of India.

questions.q: One Fundamental Duty requires citizens to respect the national flag and which other national symbol?
questions.explanation: Citizens are duty-bound to respect the Constitution, the national flag and the national anthem.

questions.q: Which of the following is listed among the Fundamental Duties?
options: To serve in the armed forces
To protect and improve the natural environment
questions.explanation: Protecting and improving the natural environment, including forests and wildlife, is a Fundamental Duty.

questions.q: Are the Fundamental Duties enforceable by the courts in the same way as Fundamental Rights?
options: No, they are not directly enforceable
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties are not directly enforceable by the courts.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties were inspired in part by the constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The idea of Fundamental Duties was drawn largely from the constitution of the former USSR.

questions.q: One Fundamental Duty calls upon citizens to cherish and follow the noble ideals that inspired which national movement?
questions.explanation: Citizens are to cherish and follow the noble ideals that inspired the national struggle for freedom.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties were added on the recommendation of which committee?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties were introduced on the recommendation of the Swaran Singh Committee in 1976.

questions.q: When the Fundamental Duties were first added in 1976, how many duties did the list originally contain?
questions.explanation: The list originally contained ten Fundamental Duties when introduced in 1976.

questions.q: The eleventh Fundamental Duty, added later, relates to a parent or guardian providing opportunities for education to a child between which ages?
questions.explanation: The eleventh duty requires providing education opportunities to a child between the ages of six and fourteen years.

questions.q: The eleventh Fundamental Duty was added by which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The eleventh duty regarding education of children was added by the 86th Amendment in 2002.

questions.q: Which of the following is included among the Fundamental Duties under Article 51A?
options: To uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India
questions.explanation: Upholding and protecting the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India is a Fundamental Duty.

questions.q: One Fundamental Duty asks citizens to value and preserve the rich heritage of the country's what?
questions.explanation: Citizens have a duty to value and preserve the rich heritage of the nation's composite culture.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Duty relates to the development of a scientific temper, humanism and the spirit of inquiry and reform?
questions.explanation: Developing scientific temper, humanism and the spirit of inquiry and reform is one of the Fundamental Duties in Article 51A.

questions.q: One Fundamental Duty requires citizens to safeguard public property and to abjure what?
questions.explanation: Citizens have a duty to safeguard public property and to abjure violence.

questions.q: A Fundamental Duty calls on citizens to promote harmony and the spirit of common brotherhood transcending diversities of religion, language, region and what else?
questions.explanation: The duty calls for harmony transcending religious, linguistic, regional and sectional diversities.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Duty relates to the natural environment, specifically including forests, lakes, rivers and wildlife?
options: The duty to defend the country
The duty to protect and improve the natural environment
questions.explanation: The duty to protect and improve the natural environment expressly includes forests, lakes, rivers and wildlife.

questions.q: One Fundamental Duty requires every citizen to strive towards excellence in all spheres of which activity?
questions.explanation: Citizens are to strive towards excellence in all spheres of individual and collective activity.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Duty relates to the defence of the country and rendering national service when called upon?
questions.explanation: Defending the country and rendering national service when called upon is one of the Fundamental Duties.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duty to have compassion for living creatures is connected in the list with respect for which of the following?
options: Living creatures and the natural environment
questions.explanation: One Fundamental Duty requires citizens to have compassion for living creatures, linked to environmental protection.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties drew partly on the report of a committee whose recommendations led to the 42nd Amendment; that committee was chaired by whom?
questions.explanation: The committee that recommended Fundamental Duties was chaired by Sardar Swaran Singh.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Duty requires citizens to abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions?
questions.explanation: The first listed Fundamental Duty is to abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties resemble Directive Principles in that both are which of the following?
questions.explanation: Like the Directive Principles, the Fundamental Duties are non-justiciable and not enforceable by courts.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Duty requires citizens to renounce practices derogatory to the dignity of which group?
questions.explanation: One Fundamental Duty calls upon citizens to renounce practices derogatory to the dignity of women.

questions.q: Fundamental Duties were introduced during the period in which a national Emergency was in force; in which year were they added?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties were added in 1976 through the 42nd Amendment.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties are sometimes contrasted with Fundamental Rights because, unlike rights, the duties carry no direct what?
questions.explanation: Unlike Fundamental Rights, the duties carry no direct legal sanction for their violation.

questions.q: Which of the following Fundamental Duties was NOT part of the original 1976 list but was added subsequently?
options: Providing education opportunities to a child aged six to fourteen
Cherishing the ideals of the freedom struggle
questions.explanation: The duty regarding education of children aged six to fourteen was added in 2002, after the original 1976 list.
